title: dianthus serratus var. scaber
intro: Dianthus serratus var. scaber, also known as the lesser fringed pink, is a type of flowering plant in the carnation family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues. The herb can be prepared in various forms, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
